{{Infobox concept
| name = Awakening
| introduced = Of Sea and Shadow
}}

''See also: [[Awakening Timeline]]''

== Overview ==
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings|Awakening is a mysterious and poorly understood procedure used to transform inanimate objects into powerful, semi-conscious items. This permanent process involves blending the [[Intent]] of the object, its owner, and a [[Reader]] to grant the item a rudimentary form of awareness or [[life]]. While an [[Awakened object]] cannot move independently, it gains a consciousness tied to its original purpose and can utilize its stored [[Intent]] to accomplish specific goals. The procedure often results in a physical transformation, altering the object's appearance or structure to better align with its purpose; however, this change occurs only once and cannot be undone. Successful Awakening requires significant time, strength, and a deep understanding of the object's goals and [[Intent]]. The difficulty is often influenced by the significance of the item to both the owner and the Reader. If a process is interrupted or attempted improperly, it can lead to dangerous outcomes, such as the object Awakening on its own without guidance or becoming unpredictable. Beyond mere utility, Awakening can be used to create [[Soulbound]] connections between a person and an object, allowing the individual to draw power from the item. Despite its importance in creating advanced weaponry and tools, the true nature of Awakening remains a subject of intense study and debate among even the most learned scholars.}}

== Of Sea and Shadow ==
<div class="wv-facts mw-collapsible mw-collapsed"><div class="wv-facts-head mw-collapsible-toggle">Sourced facts <span class="wv-facts-count">4</span></div>
<ul class="wv-fact-list mw-collapsible-content">
<li>{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[Awakening]] is the process of bringing a measure of awareness or '[[life]]' to an object.<ref name="ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow-c10">''Of Sea and Shadow'', chapter 10</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[Awakening]] is a poorly understood process used to create [[Soulbound]] and many powerful weapons.<ref name="ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow-c15">''Of Sea and Shadow'', chapter 15</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[Awakening]] is a dangerous process.<ref name="ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow-c19">''Of Sea and Shadow'', chapter 19</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[Awakening]] an object is a process that can take days or weeks.<ref name="ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow-c24">''Of Sea and Shadow'', chapter 24</ref>}}</li>
</ul></div>

== Of Shadow and Sea ==
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|Awakening is an empowering process used to change the physical nature of an object through [[Intent]]. While [[Emperor (Elder Empire)|the Emperor]] utilizes this method on gravel, it is also used by a [[Reader]] to bring an object to [[life]]. This permanent procedure can grant a weapon new powers, though it simultaneously makes a blade more powerful and less predictable. Because an Awakening can cause a blade to gain its own guidance if the [[Reading]] is not properly managed, the process is not yet fully understood.}}

<div class="wv-facts mw-collapsible mw-collapsed"><div class="wv-facts-head mw-collapsible-toggle">Sourced facts <span class="wv-facts-count">8</span></div>
<ul class="wv-fact-list mw-collapsible-content">
<li>{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|[[Awakening]] is a process [[Emperor (Elder Empire)|the Emperor]] uses on gravel.<ref name="ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea-c9">''Of Shadow and Sea'', chapter 9</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|[[Awakening]] is a process that allows an object's physical nature to be changed by [[Intent]].<ref name="ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea-c14">''Of Shadow and Sea'', chapter 14</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|[[Awakening]] is a process that is not yet fully understood.<ref name="ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea-c17">''Of Shadow and Sea'', chapter 17</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|[[Awakening]] is a permanent process that makes a blade more powerful but less predictable.<ref name="ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea-c17" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|[[Awakening]] is the process of bringing an object to [[life]] through a [[Reader]].<ref name="ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea-c19">''Of Shadow and Sea'', chapter 19</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|An [[Awakening]] is a process where a blade can gain its own guidance if not properly managed during [[Reading]].<ref name="ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea-c19" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|[[Awakening]] is a process used to empower objects.<ref name="ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea-c19" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|[[Awakening]] can grant a weapon powers it did not previously possess.<ref name="ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea-c25">''Of Shadow and Sea'', chapter 25</ref>}}</li>
</ul></div>

== Of Dawn and Darkness ==
<div class="wv-facts mw-collapsible mw-collapsed"><div class="wv-facts-head mw-collapsible-toggle">Sourced facts <span class="wv-facts-count">1</span></div>
<ul class="wv-fact-list mw-collapsible-content">
<li>{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|[[Awakening]] is a phenomenon related to the powers of a [[Soulbound]].<ref name="ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness-c7">''Of Dawn and Darkness'', chapter 7</ref>}}</li>
</ul></div>

== Of Kings and Killers ==
<div class="wv-facts mw-collapsible mw-collapsed"><div class="wv-facts-head mw-collapsible-toggle">Sourced facts <span class="wv-facts-count">4</span></div>
<ul class="wv-fact-list mw-collapsible-content">
<li>{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Awakening]] is a process of blending [[Intent]] from the object, its owner, and the Reader.<ref name="ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers-c17">''Of Kings and Killers'', chapter 17</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Awakening]] can be used to cover up imperfections in craftsmanship.<ref name="ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers-c18">''Of Kings and Killers'', chapter 18</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|No one, except perhaps [[Emperor (Elder Empire)|the Emperor]], has ever been sure exactly how [[Awakening]] works.<ref name="ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers-c18" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|If an object is significant to both the Reader and its owner, [[Awakening]] is more likely to succeed.<ref name="ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers-c25">''Of Kings and Killers'', chapter 25</ref>}}</li>
</ul></div>

== Of Killers and Kings ==
<div class="wv-facts mw-collapsible mw-collapsed"><div class="wv-facts-head mw-collapsible-toggle">Sourced facts <span class="wv-facts-count">1</span></div>
<ul class="wv-fact-list mw-collapsible-content">
<li>{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings|The [[Awakening]] process involves [[Reader]]-[[Gardener]] pairs entering secluded chambers.<ref name="ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings-c22">''Of Killers and Kings'', chapter 22</ref>}}</li>
</ul></div>

== Notes ==
<references />

{{Elder Empire nav}}

[[Category:Concepts]]
[[Category:Of Sea and Shadow]]
[[Category:Of Dawn and Darkness]]
[[Category:Of Kings and Killers]]
[[Category:Of Shadow and Sea]]
[[Category:Of Killers and Kings]]
[[Category:Elder Empire]]
[[Category:Elder Empire concepts]]
[[Category:Terminology]]
